title: begonia atroglandulosa subsp. atroglandulosa
intro: Begonia atroglandulosa subsp. atroglandulosa is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Begoniaceae, native to the Philippines. It is a shrub-like plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory and digestive issues. In traditional herbal preparations, Begonia atroglandulosa subsp. atroglandulosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
